content: All existing comparisons between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and hand-designed curious agents overlook a critical, untested moderator: the scale and structure of the agent’s long-term episodic memory archive. Curiosity signal quality in all these methods depends not just on how prediction error or surprise is calculated, but on which past memory traces the agent can draw on to contextualize new, surprising observations.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
